raw_consumer_statement:
I am filing a complaint against American Express for misrepresenting their credit card dispute policy in writing and attempting to prevent me from disputing unauthorized subscription charges from a merchant called XXXXXXXX XXXX. 

Drift is a subscription company that began charging my Amex card monthly without clear consent, login credentials, or any email receipts. I never knowingly enrolled in a subscription, and the company makes it impossible to cancel. I reached out to Amex to dispute the charges, including some dating back to late XXXX. 

An Amex customer service agent falsely told me via chat that the dispute window is limited to XXXX  days, which contradicts American Expresss actual policy that allows disputes up to XXXX  days in many cases especially involving recurring billing, fraud, or deceptive practices. I provided evidence of the merchants misrepresentation, but the agent still insisted the window was only XXXX  days, which is untrue and misleading. 

I have saved screenshots of the conversation where Amex misrepresented their policy and attempted to close the case before initiating a proper dispute investigation. This is unacceptable and violates my consumer rights under the Truth in Lending Act. 

I want American Express to acknowledge and correct the misrepresentation of their dispute timeline. I would recommend they take back their interest on all charges on this card between XX/XX/year> and now as an XXXX branch. 
I request they reopen and fully process my dispute with XXXX XXXX, including charges older than XXXX  days, as part of a recurring fraudulent pattern. 
I also request that Amex provide accurate policy information to all consumers going forward and investigate the agents conduct in misleading cardholders about their rights.
